- [creator](http://purl.org/dc/terms/creator) _:genid2147487986 

- [description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Models (PBPK) are widely used in both pharmaceutical and environmental domain by experts to assess the kinetics of xenobiotics. Thousands of papers have been published over years but the major drawback being utilization of inconsistent vocabulary which reduce the potential for automation. Machine-readable semantic technology like ontology can be used to improve the reporting of PBPK models. Currently there are multiple ontologies related to life science domain on portals like Bioportal, still there is no dedicated ontology for describing PBPK. The objective of this work is to develop a dedicated ontology for PBPK named PBPKO which can be used in future by wider kinetic community. PBPKO has covered possible mechanisms, existing kinetic terms in multiple databases and commonly utilized terminology by experts. This is freely available ontology with first version (V1.0) being published following FAIR guidelines using ROBOT tool and published at OBO foundry (crispae.github.io/pbpko/ (github.com)). It will be further enriched by including multiple species and much more kinetic terms with the help of experts. PBPKO annotates both physiological and biochemical parameters related to PBPK modeling. This ontology can be further used for text mining of PBPK parameters and model output." 

- [description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Models ontology for describing PBPK models, parameters, and processes." 

- [license](http://purl.org/dc/terms/license) [<https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/>](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/) 

- [modified](http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified) "2026-02-16"^^[date](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#date) 

- [title](http://purl.org/dc/terms/title) "PBPK Ontology" 

- [title](http://purl.org/dc/terms/title) "PBPK ontology" 

- [versionInfo](http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#versionInfo) "2026-07-10" 

- [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "PBPK Ontology"

#### Added
- Individual: [G1: Well differentiated](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002206) 

- [G1: Well differentiated](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002206) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A histologic grade according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor cells and the organization of the tumor tissue appear close to normal."@en 

- [G1: Well differentiated](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002206) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

- [G1: Well differentiated](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002206) Type [histologic grade according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002205) 


### G2 (Fuhrman) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002212`

#### Added
- Individual: [G2 (Fuhrman)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002212) 

- [G2 (Fuhrman)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002212) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A histologic grade according to the Fuhrman Nuclear Grading System indicating that nuclei are slightly irregular, approximately 15um and nucleoli are evident."@en 

- [G2 (Fuhrman)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002212) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

### G2 (WHO) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002221`

#### Added
- Individual: [G2 (WHO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002221) 

- [G2 (WHO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002221) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A histologic grade for ovarian tumor according to the World Health Organization indicating that the tumor is moderately differentiated."@en

- Individual: [Pacific Biosciences](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001856) 

- [Pacific Biosciences](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001856) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An organization that supplies tools for studying the synthesis and regulation of DNA, RNA and protein. It developed a powerful technology platform called single molecule real-time (SMRT) technology which enables real-time analysis of biomolecules with single molecule resolution."@en 

- [Pacific Biosciences](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001856) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Person: Jie Zheng" 

- [Pacific Biosciences](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001856) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Pacific Biosciences" 

- [Pacific Biosciences](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001856) Type [manufacturer](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000835)

### Rules 

#### Added
- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131)(?x, ?y), [causally downstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002404)(?x, ?y) -> [Nothing](http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#Nothing)(?y), [Nothing](http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#Nothing)(?x) 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "'causally downstream of' and 'overlaps' should be disjoint properties (a SWRL rule is required because these are non-simple properties)." 

- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131)(?x, ?y), [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411)(?x, ?y) -> [Nothing](http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#Nothing)(?y), [Nothing](http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#Nothing)(?x) 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "'causally upstream of' and 'overlaps' should be disjoint properties (a SWRL rule is required because these are non-simple properties)." 

- [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025)(?mf, ?eff), [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233)(?mf, ?in) -> [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233)(?eff, ?in) 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Input of effector is input of its parent MF" 

- [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025)(?mf, ?eff), [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233)(?eff, ?in) -> [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233)(?mf, ?in) 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "effector input is compound function input" 

- [directly negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002630)(?mf, ?mf2), [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025)(?mf, ?eff) -> [directly negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002630)(?eff, ?mf2) 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "if effector directly negatively regulates X,  its parent MF directly negatively regulates X" 

- [input of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002352)(?B, ?C), [enabled by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002333)(?A, ?B), [has negative regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002014)(?D, ?C) -> [directly negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002630)(?A, ?D) 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "inferring direct neg reg edge from input to regulatory subfunction" 

- [input of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002352)(?B, ?C), [enabled by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002333)(?A, ?B), [has positive regulatory component activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002015)(?D, ?C) -> [directly positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002629)(?A, ?D) 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "inferring direct positive reg edge from input to regulatory subfunction" 

- [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015)(?w), [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180)(?w, ?p), [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015)(?p) -> [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018)(?w, ?p) 

- [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016)(?d), [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053)(?e, ?d) -> [has disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000091)(?e, ?d) 

- [quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000019)(?d), [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053)(?e, ?d) -> [has quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000086)(?e, ?d) 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023)(?d), [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053)(?e, ?d) -> [has role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000087)(?e, ?d) 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034)(?d), [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053)(?e, ?d) -> [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085)(?e, ?d) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211)(?p, ?q), [indirectly causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0012011)(?p, ?q) -> [indirectly regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0012012)(?p, ?q) 

- [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212)(?x, ?y), [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213)(?y, ?z) -> [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212)(?x, ?z) 

- [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212)(?x, ?y), [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002)(?y, ?z) -> [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001)(?x, ?z) 

- [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212)(?y, ?z), [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213)(?x, ?y) -> [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212)(?x, ?z) 

- [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411)(?p, ?q), [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411)(?q, ?u) -> [indirectly causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0012011)(?p, ?u) 

- [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411)(?y, ?z), [acts upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002264)(?x, ?y) -> [acts upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002263)(?x, ?z) 

- [directly regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002578)(?a1, ?a2), [kinase activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0016301)(?a1), [enabled by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002333)(?a1, ?g1), [enabled by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002333)(?a2, ?g2) -> [phosphorylates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002447)(?g1, ?g2)

### Stage 0 (AJCC 7th) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002316`

#### Added

- [Stage 0 (AJCC 7th)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002316) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A clinical tumor stage group according to AJCC 7th edition indicating a carcinoma in situ (or melanoma in situ for melanoma of the skin or germ cell neoplasia in situ for testicular germ cell tumors) and generally is considered to have no metastatic potential."@en

### Stage IA (FIGO) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002327`

#### Added
- Individual: [Stage IA (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002327) 

- [Stage IA (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002327) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An International Federation of Gynecology and Obstetrics cervical cancer stage value specification indicating invasive carcinoma which can be diagnosed only by microscopy, with deepest invasion &lt;5 mm and the largest extension &lt;7 mm."@en 

- [Stage IA (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002327) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [Stage IA (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002327) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Stage IA (FIGO)" 

- [Stage IA (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002327) Type [International Federation of Gynecology and Obstetrics cervical cancer stage value specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002326) 


### Stage IA1 (FIGO) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002328`

#### Added
- Individual: [Stage IA1 (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002328) 

- [Stage IA1 (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002328) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An International Federation of Gynecology and Obstetrics cervical cancer stage value specification indicating measured stromal invasion of &lt;3.0 mm in depth and extension of &lt;7.0 mm."@en 

- [Stage IA1 (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002328) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [Stage IA1 (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002328) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Stage IA1 (FIGO)" 

- [Stage IA1 (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002328) Type [International Federation of Gynecology and Obstetrics cervical cancer stage value specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002326) 


### Stage IA2 (FIGO) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002329`

- Individual: [Stage IA2 (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002329)

### Stage IIIB (FIGO) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002338`

#### Added
- Individual: [Stage IIIB (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002338) 

- [Stage IIIB (FIGO)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002338) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An International Federation of Gynecology and Obstetrics cervical cancer stage value specification indicating extension to the pelvic wall and/or hydronephrosis or non-functioning kidney."@en

### acts upstream of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002263`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [acts upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002263) 

- [acts upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002263) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "c acts upstream of p if and only if c enables some f that is involved in p' and p' occurs chronologically before p, is not part of p, and affects the execution of p. c is a material entity and f, p, p' are processes." 

- [acts upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002263) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "acts upstream of" 

- [acts upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002263) SubPropertyOf: [acts upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002264) 

- [enables](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002327) o [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) SubPropertyOf: [acts upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002263)

### biological_process `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150`

#### Added
- Class: [biological_process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150) 

- [biological_process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A biological process is the execution of a genetically-encoded biological module or program. It consists of all the steps required to achieve the specific biological objective of the module. A biological process is accomplished by a particular set of molecular functions carried out by specific gene products (or macromolecular complexes), often in a highly regulated manner and in a particular temporal sequence." 

- [biological_process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "biological process"@en 

- [biological_process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "biological_process" 

- [biological_process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150) SubClassOf [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [biological_process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150) SubClassOf [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) some [molecular_function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0003674)

### capable of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002215`

- ObjectProperty: [capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002215) 

- [capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002215) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relation between a material entity (such as a cell) and a process, in which the material entity has the ability to carry out the process. "

- [capable of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002215) Range [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015)

### causal relation between material entity and a process `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [causal relation between material entity and a process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595) 

- [causal relation between material entity and a process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relationship that holds between a material entity and a process in which causality is involved, with either the material entity or some part of the material entity exerting some influence over the process, or the process influencing some aspect of the material entity." 

- [causal relation between material entity and a process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-6601-2165](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [causal relation between material entity and a process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "causal relation between material entity and a process" 

- [causal relation between material entity and a process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595) Domain [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) 

- [causal relation between material entity and a process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595) Range [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [causal relation between material entity and a process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002595) SubPropertyOf: [causally related to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002410) 


### causal relation between processes `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [causal relation between processes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501) 

- [causal relation between processes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p is causally related to q if and only if p or any part of p and q or any part of q are linked by a chain of events where each event pair is one where the execution of p influences the execution of q. p may be upstream, downstream, part of, or a container of q." 

- [causal relation between processes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-6601-2165](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [causal relation between processes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "causal relation between processes" 

- [causal relation between processes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501) Domain [occurrent](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003) 

- [causal relation between processes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501) Range [occurrent](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003) 

- [causal relation between processes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501) SubPropertyOf: [causally related to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002410)

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [causally influences](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002566) 

- [causally influences](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002566) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The entity or characteristic A is causally upstream of the entity or characteristic B, A having an effect on B. An entity corresponds to any biological type of entity as long as a mass is measurable. A characteristic corresponds to a particular specificity of an entity (e.g., phenotype, shape, size)."

### causally upstream of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) 

- [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p is causally upstream of q iff p is causally related to q, the end of p precedes the end of q, and p is not an occurrent part of q." 

- [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-6601-2165](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165)

- [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) SubPropertyOf: [precedes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000063) 

- [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) SubPropertyOf: [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) 

-  Transitive: [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) 


### causally upstream of or within `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) 

- [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p is 'causally upstream or within' q iff p is causally related to q, and the end of p precedes, or is coincident with, the end of q." 

- [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-6601-2165](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "causally upstream of or within" 

- [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) InverseOf [causally downstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002427) 

- [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) SubPropertyOf: [causal relation between processes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002501) 

- [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) o [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) SubPropertyOf: [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418) 

-  Transitive: [causally upstream of or within](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002418)

### causally upstream of, negative effect `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002305`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [causally upstream of, negative effect](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002305) 

- [causally upstream of, negative effect](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002305) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p is causally upstream of, negative effect q iff p is casually upstream of q, and the execution of p decreases the execution of q."

- [causally upstream of, negative effect](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002305) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "causally upstream of, negative effect" 

- [causally upstream of, negative effect](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002305) SubPropertyOf: [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) 

- [causally upstream of, negative effect](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002305) SubPropertyOf: [causally upstream of or within, negative effect](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0004046) 


### causally upstream of, positive effect `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002304`

### cellular anatomical entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165`

#### Added
- Class: [cellular anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) 

- [cellular anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A part of a cellular organism that is either an immaterial entity or a material entity with granularity above the level of a protein complex but below that of an anatomical system. Or, a substance produced by a cellular organism with granularity above the level of a protein complex." 

- [cellular anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cellular anatomical entity" 

- [cellular anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) SubClassOf [cellular_component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005575) 

- [cellular anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) SubClassOf [material anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000465) 

- [cellular anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) SubClassOf [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) some [molecule](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000013)

### cellular_component `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005575`

#### Added
- Class: [cellular_component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005575) 

- [cellular_component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005575) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A location, relative to cellular compartments and structures, occupied by a macromolecular machine. There are three types of cellular components described in the gene ontology: (1) the cellular anatomical entity where a gene product carries out a molecular function (e.g., plasma membrane, cytoskeleton) or membrane-enclosed compartments (e.g., mitochondrion); (2) virion components, where viral proteins act, and (3) the stable macromolecular complexes of which gene product are parts (e.g., the clathrin complex)." 

- [cellular_component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005575) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cellular_component" 

- [cellular_component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005575) EquivalentTo [protein-containing complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0032991) or [virion component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0044423) or [cellular anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0110165) 

- [cellular_component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005575) SubClassOf [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040)

### characteristic of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) 

- [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "inheres in"@en 

- [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this fragility is a characteristic of this vase"@en 

- [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this red color is a characteristic of this apple"@en 

- [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between a specifically dependent continuant (the characteristic) and any other entity (the bearer), in which the characteristic depends on the bearer for its existence."@en 

- [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "inheres_in"@en 

- [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "characteristic of"@en 

-  Functional: [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) 

- [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) InverseOf [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) 

- [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) SubPropertyOf: [characteristic of part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002314)

### class discovery data transformation `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175`

#### Added
- Class: [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) 

- [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A class discovery data transformation (sometimes called unsupervised classification) is a data transformation that has objective class discovery."@en 

- [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone" 

- [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "class discovery data transformation"@en 

- [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) EquivalentTo ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [clustered data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000648)) or ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178)) 

- [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) SubClassOf [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) 


### class discovery objective `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178`

#### Added
- Class: [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178) 

- [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A class discovery objective (sometimes called unsupervised classification) is a data transformation objective where the aim is to organize input data  (typically vectors of attributes) into classes, where the number of classes and their specifications are not known a priori. Depending on usage, the class assignment can be definite or probabilistic."@en 

- [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "class discovery objective"@en 

- [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178) SubClassOf [data transformation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200166) 


### class prediction data transformation `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663`

#### Added
- Class: [class prediction data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663) 

- [class prediction data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A class prediction data transformation (sometimes called supervised classification) is a data transformation that has objective class prediction."@en 

- [class prediction data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone" 

- [class prediction data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "class prediction data transformation"@en 

- [class prediction data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663) EquivalentTo ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [classified data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000023)) or ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class prediction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200179)) 

- [class prediction data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663) SubClassOf [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) 


### class prediction objective `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200179`

#### Added
- Class: [class prediction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200179) 

- [class prediction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200179) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A class prediction objective (sometimes called supervised classification) is a data transformation objective where the aim is to create a predictor from training data through a machine learning technique. The training data consist of pairs of objects (typically vectors of attributes) and
class labels for these objects. The resulting predictor can be used to attach class labels to any valid novel input object. Depending on usage, the prediction can be definite or probabilistic. A classification is learned from the training data and can then be tested on test data."@en 

- [class prediction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200179) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [class prediction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200179) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "class prediction objective"@en 

- [class prediction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200179) SubClassOf [data transformation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200166) 


### classified data set `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000023`

- [classified data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000023) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Monnie McGee" 

- [classified data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000023) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "classified data set"@en 

- [classified data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000023) EquivalentTo [is specified output of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000312) some [class prediction data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663) 

- [classified data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000023) SubClassOf [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) 

- [classified data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000023) SubClassOf [is specified output of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000312) some [class prediction data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000663)

- [complex of molecules](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000080) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A complex of two or more molecules that are not covalently bound."@en 

- [complex of molecules](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000080) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "complex of molecules"@en 

- [complex of molecules](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000080) SubClassOf [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) 

- [complex of molecules](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000080) SubClassOf [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) some [molecule](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000013)

### concretizes `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000059`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [concretizes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000059) 

- [concretizes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000059) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relationship between a specifically dependent continuant and a generically dependent continuant, in which the generically dependent continuant depends on some independent continuant in virtue of the fact that the specifically dependent continuant also depends on that same independent continuant. Multiple specifically dependent continuants can concretize the same generically dependent continuant."@en 

- [concretizes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000059) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "concretizes"@en 


### continuant `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002`

#### Added
- Class: [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) 

- [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An entity that exists in full at any time in which it exists at all, persists through time while maintaining its identity and has no temporal parts."@en 

- [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: Continuant entities are entities which can be sliced to yield parts only along the spatial dimension, yielding for example the parts of your table which we call its legs, its top, its nails. &lsquo;My desk stretches from the window to the door. It has spatial parts, and can be sliced (in space) in two. With respect to time, however, a thing is a continuant.&rsquo; [60, p. 240"@en 

- [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "continuant"@en 

- [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) DisjointWith [occurrent](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003) 

- [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) DisjointWith [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some [occurrent](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003) 

- [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) SubClassOf [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) 

- [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) only [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002)

### cross validation objective `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200188`

#### Added
- Class: [cross validation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200188) 

- [cross validation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200188) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A cross validation objective is a data transformation objective in which the aim is to partition a sample of data into subsets such that the analysis is initially performed on a single subset, while the other subset(s) are retained for subsequent use in confirming and validating the initial analysis."@en 

- [cross validation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200188) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [cross validation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200188) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "cross validation objective"@en 

- [cross validation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200188) SubClassOf [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172)

### curation status specification `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078`

#### Added
- Class: [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "curation status specification"@en 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125) 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The curation status of the term. The allowed values come from an enumerated list of predefined terms. See the specification of these instances for more detailed definitions of each enumerated value."@en 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "Better to represent curation as a process with parts and then relate labels to that process (in IAO meeting)"@en 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON:Bill Bug"@en 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "GROUP:OBI:&lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/obi&gt;"@en 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "OBI_0000266"@en 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "curation status specification"@en 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) EquivalentTo {[example to be eventually removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000002) , [metadata complete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120) , [organizational term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000121) , [ready for release](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122) , [metadata incomplete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000123) , [uncurated](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000124) , [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125) , [to be replaced with external ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000423) , [requires discussion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000428)} 

- [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) SubClassOf [data about an ontology part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000102)

### data collection `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000`

#### Added
- Class: [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) 

- [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "data collection"@en 

- [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A data entity that consists of multiple data entities."@en 

- [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Sebastian Duesing" 

- [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "data set"@en 

- [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "https://github.com/information-artifact-ontology/IAO/issues/283" 

- [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "https://github.com/obi-ontology/obi/issues/1877" 

- [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "data collection"@en 

- [data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0001000) SubClassOf [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) 


### data entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027`

#### Added
- Class: [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "data entity"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Data items include counts of things, analyte concentrations, and statistical summaries."@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125) 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An information content entity that is intended to be a truthful statement about something (modulo, e.g., measurement precision or other systematic errors) and is constructed/acquired by a method which reliably tends to produce (approximately) truthful statements."@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An information content entity that is intended to be one or more truthful statement(s) about something (modulo, e.g., measurement precision or other systematic errors) and is constructed/acquired by a method which reliably tends to produce (approximately) truthful statements."@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2/2/2009 Alan and Bjoern discussing FACS run output data. This is a data item because it is about the cell population. Each element records an event and is typically further composed a set of measurment data items that record the fluorescent intensity stimulated by one of the lasers."@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2009-03-16: data item deliberatly ambiguous: we merged data set and datum to be one entity, not knowing how to define singular versus plural. So data item is more general than datum."@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2009-03-16: removed datum as alternative term as datum specifically refers to singular form, and is thus not an exact synonym."@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2014-03-31: See discussion at http://odontomachus.wordpress.com/2014/03/30/aboutness-objects-propositions/" 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "JAR: datum     -- well, this will be very tricky to define, but maybe some 
information-like stuff that might be put into a computer and that is 
meant, by someone, to denote and/or to be interpreted by some 
process... I would include lists, tables, sentences... I think I might 
defer to Barry, or to Brian Cantwell Smith

JAR: A data item is an approximately justified approximately true approximate belief"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Chris Stoeckert"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Jonathan Rees"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "data item"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "data"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "data entity"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "data item"@en 

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) SubClassOf [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) 


### data format specification `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000098`

#### Added
- Class: [data format specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000098) 

- [data format specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000098) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A data format specification is the information content borne by the document published defining the specification.
Example: The ISO document specifying what encompasses an XML document; The instructions in a XSD file"@en 

- [data format specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000098) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "data format specification"@en 

- [data format specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000098) SubClassOf [directive information entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000033)

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Melanie Courtot"@en 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Philippe Rocca-Serra" 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Richard Scheuermann"@en 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Ryan Brinkman"@en 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Tina Hernandez-Boussard"@en 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "data transformation"@en 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) EquivalentTo [achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [data transformation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200166) 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) SubClassOf [completely executed planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000035) 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) SubClassOf [has specified input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000293) only [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) SubClassOf ([has specified input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000293) some [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027)) and ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027)) 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) SubClassOf [realizes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000055) some ([concretizes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000059) some 
([algorithm](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000064) and ([has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) some [objective specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000005)))) 

- [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) SubClassOf [achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [data transformation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200166)

### decision tree building data transformation `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000707`

#### Added

- [decision tree building data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000707) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A decision tree building data transformation is a data transformation that has objective decision tree induction."@en 

- [decision tree building data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000707) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone" 

- [decision tree building data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000707) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "decision tree building data transformation"@en 

- [decision tree building data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000707) EquivalentTo [achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [decision tree induction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000704) 

- [decision tree building data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000707) SubClassOf [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000) 

- [decision tree building data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000707) SubClassOf [achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [decision tree induction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000704) 


### decision tree induction objective `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000704`

#### Added
- Class: [decision tree induction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000704) 

- [decision tree induction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000704) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A decision tree induction objective is a data transformation objective in which a tree-like graph of edges and nodes is created and from which the selection of each branch requires that some type of logical decision is made."@en 

- [decision tree induction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000704) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone" 

- [decision tree induction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000704) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "decision tree induction objective" 

- [decision tree induction objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000704) SubClassOf [data transformation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200166) 


### defined class `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420`

#### Added
- Individual: [defined class](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420) 

- [defined class](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "defined class"@en 

- [defined class](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A defined class is a class that is defined by a set of logically necessary and sufficient conditions but is not a universal"@en 

- [defined class](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "&quot;definitions&quot;, in some readings, always are given by necessary and sufficient conditions. So one must be careful (and this is difficult sometimes) to distinguish between defined classes and universal."@en 

- [defined class](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [defined class](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "defined class"@en 

- [defined class](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420) Type [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409)

Free text indicate / attribute source(s) for the definition. EXAMPLE: Author Name, URI, MeSH Term C04, PUBMED ID, Wiki uri on 31.01.2007"@en 

- [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON:Daniel Schober"@en

### degree Celsius `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000027`

- [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "denotator type"@en 

- [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "The Basic Formal Ontology ontology makes a distinction between Universals and defined classes, where the formal are &quot;natural kinds&quot; and the latter arbitrary collections of entities."@en 

- [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A denotator type indicates how a term should be interpreted from an ontological perspective."@en

- [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "denotator type"@en 

- [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409) EquivalentTo {[universal](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000410) , [defined class](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000420) , [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421)} 

- [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409) SubClassOf [data about an ontology part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000102)

### dimensionality reduction `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050`

#### Added
- Class: [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) 

- [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A dimensionality reduction is data partitioning which transforms each input m-dimensional vector (x_1, x_2, ..., x_m) into an output n-dimensional vector (y_1, y_2, ..., y_n), where n is smaller than m."@en 

- [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Elisabetta Manduchi"@en 

- [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Melanie Courtot"@en 

- [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Philippe Rocca-Serra" 

- [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "dimensionality reduction"@en 

- [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) SubClassOf [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) 

- [dimensionality reduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200050) SubClassOf ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [clustered data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000648)) and ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178))

### directly regulates activity of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [directly regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448) 

- [directly regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The entity A, immediately upstream of the entity B, has an activity that regulates an activity performed by B. For example, A and B may be gene products and binding of B by A regulates the kinase activity of B.

A and B can be physically interacting but not necessarily. Immediately upstream means there are no intermediate entity between A and B." 

- [directly regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-6601-2165](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [directly regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0003-4639-4431](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4639-4431)

- [directly regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448) Domain [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) 

- [directly regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448) Range [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) 

- [directly regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448) SubPropertyOf: [molecularly interacts with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002436) 

- [directly regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002448) SubPropertyOf: [regulates activity of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0011002)

### disposition `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016`

#### Added
- Class: [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) 

- [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an atom of element X has the disposition to decay to an atom of element Y"@en 

- [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "certain people have a predisposition to colon cancer"@en 

- [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "children are innately disposed to categorize objects in certain ways."@en 

- [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the cell wall is disposed to filter chemicals in endocytosis and exocytosis"@en 

- [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: Dispositions exist along a strength continuum. Weaker forms of disposition are realized in only a fraction of triggering cases. These forms occur in a significant number of cases of a similar type."@en 

- [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "disposition"@en 

- [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) DisjointWith [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023)

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The term was added to the ontology on the assumption it was a valid domain entity, but it turns out the entity does not exist in reality."@en 

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "This obsolesence reason should be used conservatively. For example: Obsoleting class that describes a breed of cow based on a record in an existing database, that was later retracted as faulty (breed does not exist). Do not use this term to obsolete a historic concept (that was once valid, but not anymore). "@en

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "domain entity does not exist" 

- [domain entity does not exist](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001002) Type [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225)

### entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001`

#### Added
- Class: [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Julius Caesar"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Verdi&rsquo;s Requiem"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the Second World War"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "your body mass index"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: In all areas of empirical inquiry we encounter general terms of two sorts. First are general terms which refer to universals or types:animaltuberculosissurgical procedurediseaseSecond, are general terms used to refer to groups of entities which instantiate a given universal but do not correspond to the extension of any subuniversal of that universal because there is nothing intrinsic to the entities in question by virtue of which they &ndash; and only they &ndash; are counted as belonging to the given group. Examples are: animal purchased by the Emperortuberculosis diagnosed on a Wednesdaysurgical procedure performed on a patient from Stockholmperson identified as candidate for clinical trial #2056-555person who is signatory of Form 656-PPVpainting by Leonardo da VinciSuch terms, which represent what are called &lsquo;specializations&rsquo; in [81"@en 

- [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "entity"@en

### function `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034`

#### Added
- Class: [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of a hammer to drive in nails"@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of a heart pacemaker to regulate the beating of a heart through electricity"@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of amylase in saliva to break down starch into sugar"@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: In the past, we have distinguished two varieties of function, artifactual function and biological function. These are not asserted subtypes of BFO:function however, since the same function &ndash; for example: to pump, to transport &ndash; can exist both in artifacts and in biological entities. The asserted subtypes of function that would be needed in order to yield a separate monoheirarchy are not artifactual function, biological function, etc., but rather transporting function, pumping function, etc."@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "function"@en 

- [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) SubClassOf [disposition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000016) 


### function of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this catalysis function is a function of this enzyme"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between a function and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A function inheres in its bearer at all times for which the function exists, however the function need not be realized at all the times that the function exists."@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "function_of"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "is function of"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "function of"@en 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) InverseOf [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) Domain [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) 

- [function of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079) SubPropertyOf: [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052) 


### functionally related to `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002328`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [functionally related to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002328) 

- [functionally related to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002328) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A grouping relationship for any relationship directly involving a function, or that holds because of a function of one of the related entities."

- [functionally related to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002328) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "functionally related to"@en

### generically dependent continuant `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031`

#### Added
- Class: [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "The entries in your database are patterns instantiated as quality instances in your hard drive. The database itself is an aggregate of such patterns. When you create the database you create a particular instance of the generically dependent continuant type database. Each entry in the database is an instance of the generically dependent continuant type IAO: information content entity."@en 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the pdf file on your laptop, the pdf file that is a copy thereof on my laptop"@en 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the sequence of this protein molecule; the sequence that is a copy thereof in that protein molecule."@en 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "generically dependent continuant"@en 

- [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) SubClassOf [continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000002)

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this apple is bearer of this red color"@en 

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this vase is bearer of this fragility"@en 

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Inverse of characteristic_of"@en 

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A bearer can have many dependents, and its dependents can exist for different periods of time, but none of its dependents can exist when the bearer does not exist."@en 

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "bearer_of"@en 

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "is bearer of"@en 

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has characteristic"@en 

-  InverseFunctional: [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) 

- [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) Range [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) 


### has component `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180) 

- [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "w 'has component' p if w 'has part' p and w is such that it can be directly disassembled into into n parts p, p2, p3, ..., pn, where these parts are of similar type." 

- [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has component"@en 

- [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180) SubPropertyOf: [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051)

- [has component process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018) SubPropertyOf: [has component](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002180)

### has function `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this enzyme has function this catalysis function (more colloquially: this enzyme has this catalysis function)"@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a function, in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A bearer can have many functions, and its functions can exist for different periods of time, but none of its functions can exist when the bearer does not exist. A function need not be realized at all the times that the function exists."@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "has_function"@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "has function"@en 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) Domain [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004) 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) Range [function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000034) 

- [has function](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000085) SubPropertyOf: [has characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053) 


### has input `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233`

- [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) Domain [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [has input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002233) SubPropertyOf: [has participant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057)

#### Added

### hierarchical clustering `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042`

#### Added
- Class: [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A hierarchical clustering is a data transformation which achieves a class discovery objective, which takes as input data item and builds a hierarchy of clusters. The traditional representation of this hierarchy is a tree (visualized by a dendrogram), with the individual input objects at one end (leaves) and a single cluster containing every object at the other (root)."@en 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "hierarchical clustering"@en 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) SubClassOf [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) 

- [hierarchical clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200042) SubClassOf ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [clustered data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000648)) and ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178))

### homogenous data collection `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100`

#### Added
- Class: [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "homogenous data collection"@en 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Intensity values in a CEL file or from multiple CEL files comprise a data set (as opposed to the CEL files themselves)."@en 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125) 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A data item that is an aggregate of other data items of the same type that have something in common. Averages and distributions can be determined for data sets."@en 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2009/10/23 Alan Ruttenberg. The intention is that this term represent collections of like data. So this isn't for, e.g. the whole contents of a cel file, which includes parameters, metadata etc. This is more like java arrays of a certain rather specific type"@en 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2014-05-05: Data sets are aggregates and thus must include two or more data items. We have chosen not to add logical axioms to make this restriction." 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "person:Allyson Lister"@en 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "person:Chris Stoeckert"@en 

- [homogenous data collection](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000100) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "data set"@en
