### information content entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030`

#### Added
- Class: [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "information content entity"@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "Examples of information content entites include journal articles, data, graphical layouts, and graphs."@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [ready for release](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122) 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A generically dependent continuant that is about some thing."@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2014-03-10: The use of &quot;thing&quot; is intended to be general enough to include universals and configurations (see https://groups.google.com/d/msg/information-ontology/GBxvYZCk1oc/-L6B5fSBBTQJ)."@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "information_content_entity 'is_encoded_in' some digital_entity in obi before split (040907). information_content_entity 'is_encoded_in' some physical_document in obi before split (040907).

Previous. An information content entity is a non-realizable information entity that 'is encoded in' some digital or physical entity."@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Chris Stoeckert"@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "OBI_0000142"@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "information content entity"@en 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) SubClassOf [generically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000031) 

- [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000001)

### is about `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "This document is about information artifacts and their representations"@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125) 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A (currently) primitive relation that relates an information artifact to an entity."@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "7/6/2009 Alan Ruttenberg. Following discussion with Jonathan Rees, and introduction of &quot;mentions&quot; relation. Weaken the is_about relationship to be primitive. 

We will try to build it back up by elaborating the various subproperties that are more precisely defined.

Some currently missing phenomena that should be considered &quot;about&quot; are predications - &quot;The only person who knows the answer is sitting beside me&quot; , Allegory, Satire, and other literary forms that can be topical without explicitly mentioning the topic."@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "person:Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "Smith, Ceusters, Ruttenberg, 2000 years of philosophy"@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "is about"@en 

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) Domain [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030)

### k-means clustering `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041`

#### Added
- Class: [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A k-means clustering is a data transformation which achieves a class discovery or partitioning objective, which takes as input a collection of objects (represented as points in multidimensional space) and which partitions them into a specified number k of clusters. The algorithm attempts to find the centers of natural clusters in the data. The most common form of the algorithm starts by partitioning the input points into k initial sets, either at random or using some heuristic data. It then calculates the mean point, or centroid, of each set. It constructs a new partition by associating each point with the closest centroid. Then the centroids are recalculated for the new clusters, and the algorithm repeated by alternate applications of these two steps until convergence, which is obtained when the points no longer switch clusters (or alternatively centroids are no longer changed)."@en 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Elisabetta Manduchi" 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Philippe Rocca-Serra" 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "k-means clustering"@en 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) SubClassOf [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) SubClassOf [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) 

- [k-means clustering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200041) SubClassOf ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [clustered data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000648)) and ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178)) 


### k-nearest neighbors `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727`

#### Added
- Class: [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A k-nearest neighbors is a data transformation which achieves a class discovery or partitioning objective, in which an input data object with vector y is assigned to a class label based upon the k closest training data set points to y; where k is the largest value that class label is assigned."@en 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "James Malone"@en 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "k-nearest neighbors"@en 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) SubClassOf [partitioning data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200171) 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) SubClassOf [class discovery data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200175) 

- [k-nearest neighbors](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000727) SubClassOf ([has specified output](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000299) some [clustered data set](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000648)) and ([achieves_planned_objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000417) some [class discovery objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200178))

### leave one out cross validation method `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033`

#### Added
- Class: [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) 

- [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "is a data transformation :  leave-one-out cross-validation (LOOCV) involves using a single observation from the original sample as the validation data, and the remaining observations as the training data. This is repeated such that each observation in the sample is used once as the validation data"@en 

- [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Person:Helen Parkinson" 

- [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "leave one out cross validation method"@en 

- [leave one out cross validation method](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200033) SubClassOf [statistical model validation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000792)

([Homo sapiens](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_9606) or [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245))

### material entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040`

#### Added
- Class: [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a flame"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a forest fire"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a human being"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a hurricane"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a photon"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a puff of smoke"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a sea wave"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "a tornado"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an aggregate of human beings."@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an energy wave"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "an epidemic"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the undetached arm of a human being"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An independent continuant that is spatially extended whose identity is independent of that of other entities and can be maintained through time."@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: Material entities (continuants) can preserve their identity even while gaining and losing material parts. Continuants are contrasted with occurrents, which unfold themselves in successive temporal parts or phases [60"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: Object, Fiat Object Part and Object Aggregate are not intended to be exhaustive of Material Entity. Users are invited to propose new subcategories of Material Entity."@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: &lsquo;Matter&rsquo; is intended to encompass both mass and energy (we will address the ontological treatment of portions of energy in a later version of BFO). A portion of matter is anything that includes elementary particles among its proper or improper parts: quarks and leptons, including electrons, as the smallest particles thus far discovered; baryons (including protons and neutrons) at a higher level of granularity; atoms and molecules at still higher levels, forming the cells, organs, organisms and other material entities studied by biologists, the portions of rock studied by geologists, the fossils studied by paleontologists, and so on.Material entities are three-dimensional entities (entities extended in three spatial dimensions), as contrasted with the processes in which they participate, which are four-dimensional entities (entities extended also along the dimension of time).According to the FMA, material entities may have immaterial entities as parts &ndash; including the entities identified below as sites; for example the interior (or &lsquo;lumen&rsquo;) of your small intestine is a part of your body. BFO 2.0 embodies a decision to follow the FMA here."@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "material entity"@en 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) DisjointWith [immaterial entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000141) 

- [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040) SubClassOf [independent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004)

- [metadata complete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Class has all its metadata, but is either not guaranteed to be in its final location in the asserted IS_A hierarchy or refers to another class that is not complete."@en

- [metadata complete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120) Type [curation status specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078) 

-  DifferentIndividuals: [metadata complete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000120), [organizational term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000121), [ready for release](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000122), [metadata incomplete](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000123), [uncurated](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000124), [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125), [to be replaced with external ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000423), [requires discussion](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000428)

### mutually spatially disjoint with `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171`

#### Added
- AnnotationProperty: [mutually spatially disjoint with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) 

- [mutually spatially disjoint with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A is mutually_spatially_disjoint_with B if both A and B are classes, and there exists no p such that p is part_of some A and p is part_of some B." 

- [mutually spatially disjoint with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [OMO_0002000](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0002000) "PREFIX owl: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#&gt;
PREFIX part_of: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050&gt;
PREFIX mutually_spatially_disjoint_with: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171&gt;
CONSTRUCT {
  part_of: a owl:ObjectProperty .
  [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty part_of: ;
    owl:someValuesFrom ?x ;
    owl:disjointWith [
      a owl:Restriction ;
      owl:onProperty part_of: ;
      owl:someValuesFrom ?y
    ]
  ]
}
WHERE {
  ?x mutually_spatially_disjoint_with: ?y .
}" 

- [mutually spatially disjoint with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002171) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "mutually spatially disjoint with"@en

### named class expression `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421`

#### Added
- Individual: [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "named class expression"@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A named class expression is a logical expression that is given a name. The name can be used in place of the expression."@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "named class expressions are used in order to have more concise logical definition but their extensions may not be interesting classes on their own. In languages such as OWL, with no provisions for macros, these show up as actuall classes. Tools may with to not show them as such, and to replace uses of the macros with their expansions"@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "named class expression"@en 

- [named class expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000421) Type [denotator type](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000409)

### negatively regulates characteristic `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002`

- [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) SubPropertyOf: [regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019000) 

- [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212) o [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001) SubPropertyOf: [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) o [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) SubPropertyOf: [negatively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019002) 


### never in taxon `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161`

#### Added
- AnnotationProperty: [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "x never in taxon T if and only if T is a class, and x does not instantiate the class expression &quot;in taxon some T&quot;. Note that this is a shortcut relation, and should be used as a hasValue restriction in OWL." 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-6601-2165](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [OMO_0002000](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0002000) "PREFIX rdfs: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#&gt;
PREFIX owl: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#&gt;
PREFIX in_taxon: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162&gt;
PREFIX never_in_taxon: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161&gt;
CONSTRUCT {
  in_taxon: a owl:ObjectProperty .
  ?x owl:disjointWith [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty in_taxon: ;
    owl:someValuesFrom ?taxon
  ] .
  ?x rdfs:subClassOf [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty in_taxon: ;
    owl:someValuesFrom [
      a owl:Class ;
      owl:complementOf ?taxon
    ]
  ] .
}
WHERE {
  ?x never_in_taxon: ?taxon .
}" 

- [never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "never in taxon"

#### Added
- Class: [oatp](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00218) 

- [oatp](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00218) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A transporter family (Organic Anion Transporting Polypeptides) involved in transporting organic anions." 

- [oatp](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00218) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "0000-0001-8345-1349" 

- [oatp](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00218) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "0000-0002-9795-5967" 

- [oatp](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00218) [closeMatch](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#closeMatch) "http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/TXPO_0000272" 

- [oatp](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00218) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "oatp" 

- [oatp](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00218) SubClassOf [transporter](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00217) 


### objective specification `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000005`

#### Added
- Class: [objective specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000005) 

- [objective specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000005) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A directive information entity that describes an intended process endpoint. When part of a plan specification the concretization is realized in a planned process in which the bearer tries to effect the world so that the process endpoint is achieved."@en 

- [objective specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000005) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "objective specification"@en 

- [objective specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000005) SubClassOf [directive information entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000033) 


### objective_achieved_by `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000833`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [objective_achieved_by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000833) 

- [objective_achieved_by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000833) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "This relation obtains between an objective specification and a planned process when the criteria specified in the objective specification are met at the end of the planned process."@en 

- [objective_achieved_by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000833) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "OBI" 

- [objective_achieved_by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000833) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "objective_achieved_by" 

- [objective_achieved_by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000833) Domain [objective specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000005) 

- [objective_achieved_by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000833) Range [completely executed planned process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000035) 


### obsolescence reason specification `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225`

#### Added
- Class: [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "obsolescence reason specification"@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [has curation status](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000114) [pending final vetting](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000125) 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The reason for which a term has been deprecated. The allowed values come from an enumerated list of predefined terms. See the specification of these instances for more detailed definitions of each enumerated value."@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "The creation of this class has been inspired in part by Werner Ceusters' paper, Applying evolutionary terminology auditing to the Gene Ontology."@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Alan Ruttenberg"@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Melanie Courtot"@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "obsolescence reason specification"@en 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) EquivalentTo {[failed exploratory term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000103) , [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) , [terms merged](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000227) , [term imported](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000228) , [term split](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000229) , [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000)} 

- [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225) SubClassOf [data about an ontology part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000102)

### organization `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245`

#### Added
- Class: [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An entity that can bear roles, has members, and has a set of organization rules. Members of organizations are either organizations themselves or individual people. Members can bear specific organization member roles that are determined in the organization rules. The organization rules also determine how decisions are made on behalf of the organization by the organization members."@en 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Alan Ruttenberg" 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Bjoern Peters" 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Philippe Rocca-Serra" 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "PERSON: Susanna Sansone" 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "organization"@en 

- [organization](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000245) SubClassOf [material entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000040)

### out of scope `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000`

#### Added
- Individual: [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The term was added to the ontology on the assumption it was in scope, but it turned out later that it was not."@en 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "This obsolesence reason should be used conservatively. Typical valid examples in disease ontologies are: unnecessary class grouping; mistaked creation of a disease associated to a phenotype."@en 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0001-5208-3432](http://orcid.org/0000-0001-5208-3432) 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "https://github.com/information-artifact-ontology/ontology-metadata/issues/77" 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "out of scope" 

- [out of scope](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0001000) Type [obsolescence reason specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000225)

### overlaps `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) 

- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "x overlaps y if and only if there exists some z such that x has part z and z part of y" 

- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "overlaps"@en 

- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) SubPropertyOf: [mereotopologically related to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002323) 

- [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) o [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) SubPropertyOf: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) 

- [has part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000051) o [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) SubPropertyOf: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) 

- [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) o [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) SubPropertyOf: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) 

-  Symmetric: [overlaps](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131)

- [pM1a (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002304) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic distant metastases stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that metastasis is histologically confirmed and a separate tumor nodule(s) in a contralateral lobe; tumor with pleural nodules OR malignant pleural or pericardial effusion."@en

### pT1 (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) 

- [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is 3 cm or less in greatest dimension, surrounded by lung or visceral pleura without bronchoscopic evidence of invasion more proximal than the lobar bronchus (i.e., not in the main bronchus)."@en 

- [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT1 (lung)" 

- [pT1 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002235) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002232)

- Individual: [pT1a (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002236)

- Individual: [pT1c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002261)

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002248) 

- [pT2 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002248) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 7 cm in greatest dimension and limited to the kidney."@en 

- [pT2 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002248) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

### pT2 (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) 

- [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 3 cm but not more than 7 cm or the tumor has any of the following features: involves main bronchus, 2 cm or more distal to the carina, invades visceral pleura, associated with atelectasis or obstructive pneumonitis that extends to the hilar region but does not involve the entire lung."@en 

- [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT2 (lung)" 

- [pT2 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002238) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002232) 


### pT2 (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) 

- [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor involves one or both ovaries with pelvic extension."@en 

- [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT2 (ovary)" 

- [pT2 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002262) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256)

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002249) 

- [pT2a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002249) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 7 cm but not more than 10 cm."@en 

- [pT2a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002249) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT2a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002249) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT2a (kidney)" 

- [pT2a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002249) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002243) 


### pT2a (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002239`

- Individual: [pT2a (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002239) 

- [pT2a (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002239) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 3 cm but not more than 5 cm in greatest dimension."@en 

- [pT2a (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002239) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002263) 

- [pT2a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002263) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has extension and/or implants on uterus and/or tube(s) and no malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en 

- [pT2a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002263) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

- [pT2a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002263) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256)

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002250) 

- [pT2b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002250) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 10 cm and limited to the kidney."@en 

- [pT2b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002250) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2b (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002240) 

- [pT2b (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002240) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 5 cm but not more than 7 cm in greatest dimension."@en 

- [pT2b (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002240) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002264) 

- [pT2b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002264) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has extension to other pelvic tissues and no malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en

### pT2c (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002265`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT2c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002265) 

- [pT2c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002265) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has pelvic extension with malignant cells in ascites or peritoneal washings."@en

### pT3 (colon) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) 

- [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor invades subserosa or into non-peritionealized pericolic or perirectal tissues."@en 

- [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3 (colon)" 

- [pT3 (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002229) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002224) 


### pT3 (kidney) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) 

- [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor extends into major veins or perinephric tissues but not into the ipsilateral adrenal gland and not beyond the Gerota fascia."@en 

- [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3 (kidney)" 

- [pT3 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002251) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002243) 


### pT3 (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) 

- [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor is more than 7 cm or one that directly invades any of: parietal pleura, chest wall (including superior sulcus tumors), diaphragm, phrenic nerve, mediastinal pleura, parietal pericardiu or the tumor is in the main bronchus less than 2 cm distal to the carina but without  involvement of the carina or there is associated atelectasis or obstructive pneumonitis of the entire lung or there is separate tumor nodule(s) in the same lobe as the primary."@en 

- [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3 (lung)" 

- [pT3 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002241) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002232) 


### pT3 (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) 

- [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor involves one or both ovaries with microscopically confirmed peritoneal metastasis outside the pelvis and/or regional lymph node metastasis."@en 

- [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3 (ovary)" 

- [pT3 (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002266) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256) 


### pT3a (kidney) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) 

- [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor grossly extends into the renal vein or its segmental (muscle containing) branches, or the tumor invades perirenal and/or renal sinus fat (peripelvic) fat but not beyond Gerota fascia."@en 

- [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3a (kidney)" 

- [pT3a (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002252) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002243) 


### pT3a (ovary) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) 

- [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has microscopic peritoneal metastasis beyond pelvis."@en 

- [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT3a (ovary)" 

- [pT3a (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002267) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256) 


### pT3b (kidney) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253) 

- [pT3b (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002253) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor grossly extends into vena cava below diaphragm."@en

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002268) 

- [pT3b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002268) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has macroscopic peritoneal, metastatasis beyond pelvis, 2 cm or less in greatest dimension."@en 

- [pT3b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002268) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

- [pT3b (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002268) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002256) 


### pT3c (kidney) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002254`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3c (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002254) 

- [pT3c (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002254) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor grossly extends into vena cava above the diaphragm or Invades the wall of the vena cava."@en 

- [pT3c (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002254) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

#### Added
- Individual: [pT3c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002269) 

- [pT3c (ovary)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002269) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for ovary according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor has peritoneal metastasis beyond pelvis, more than 2 cm in greatest dimension and/or regional lymph node metastasis."@en

#### Added
- Individual: [pT4 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002255) 

- [pT4 (kidney)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002255) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for kidney according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor invades beyond Gerota fascia (including contiguous extension into the ipsilateral adrenal gland)."@en

### pT4 (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242`

#### Added
- Individual: [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) 

- [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor of any size that invades any of the following: mediastinum, heart, great vessels, trachea, recurrent laryngeal nerve, esophagus, vertebral body, carina or there is separate tumor nodule(s) in a different ipsilateral lobe to that of the primary."@en 

- [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pT4 (lung)" 

- [pT4 (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002242) Type [pathologic primary tumor stage for lung according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002232)

#### Added
- Individual: [pT4a (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002230) 

- [pT4a (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002230) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor perforates visceral peritoneum."@en 

- [pT4a (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002230) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis"

- Individual: [pT4b (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002231) 

- [pT4b (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002231) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition indicating that the tumor directly invades other organs or structures."@en

- Individual: [pTis (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002226) 

- [pTis (colon)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002226) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition indicating carcinoma in situ (intraepithelial or invasion of lamina propria)."@en

### pTis (lung) `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002234`

#### Added
- Individual: [pTis (lung)](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002234)

- [partitioning objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200172) SubClassOf [data transformation objective](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200166) 


### pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290`

#### Added
- Class: [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) 

- [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A categorical value specification that is a pathologic finding about one or more characteristics of colon cancer following the rules of the TNM AJCC v7 classification system as they pertain to distant metastases. TNM pathologic distant metastasis findings are based on clinical findings supplemented by histopathologic examination of one or more tissue specimens acquired during surgery."@en 

- [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "Chris Stoeckert, Helena Ellis" 

- [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition" 

- [pathologic distant metastases stage for colon according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002290) SubClassOf [categorical value specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0001930)

### pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002224`

#### Added
- Class: [pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002224) 

- [pathologic primary tumor stage for colon and rectum according to AJCC 7th edition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002224) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A categorical value specification that is a pathologic finding about one or more characteristics of colorectal cancer following the rules of the TNM American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC) version 7 classification system as they pertain to staging of the primary tumor. TNM pathologic primary tumor findings are based on clinical findings supplemented by histopathologic examination of one or more tissue specimens acquired during surgery."@en

### peak matching `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000726`

- [peak matching](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000726) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Peak matching is a data transformation performed on a dataset of a graph of ordered data points (e.g. a spectrum) with the objective of pattern matching local maxima above a noise threshold"@en

- [peak matching](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000726) SubClassOf [data transformation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0200000)

### peptide `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670`

#### Added
- Class: [peptide](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670) 

- [peptide](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Amide derived from two or more amino carboxylic acid molecules (the same or different) by formation of a covalent bond from the carbonyl carbon of one to the nitrogen atom of another with formal loss of water. The term is usually applied to structures formed from &alpha;-amino acids, but it includes those derived from any amino carboxylic acid. X = OH, OR, NH2, NHR, etc." 

- [peptide](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "peptide" 

- [peptide](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_16670) SubClassOf [macromolecule](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_33839)

### peripheral compartment `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00321`

#### Added
- Class: [peripheral compartment](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00321) 

- [peripheral compartment](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00321) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A compartment in a multi-compartment model representing tissues where compound distribution is slower compared to the central compartment." 

- [peripheral compartment](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00321) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "0000-0001-8345-1349" 

- [peripheral compartment](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00321) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) "0000-0002-9795-5967" 

- [peripheral compartment](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00321) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "peripheral compartment" 

- [peripheral compartment](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00321) SubClassOf [compartment](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PBPKO_00446)

- [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The term was created to temporarily stand in for a semantic purpose, but is no longer needed, typically due to another permanent term being defined."@en 

- [placeholder removed](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000226) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "This reason is appropriate for a temporary term even if no permanent term is defined; for example if the need for the term no longer exists. If a permanent (replacement) term is defined, the deprecated term should reference the replacement with IAO:0100001. If no replacement is defined, it may be appropriate to reference alternative terms with oboInOwl:consider."@en

- [plan](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000260) SubClassOf [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017)

- [plan specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "plan specification"@en 

- [plan specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104) SubClassOf [directive information entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000033)

### positively regulates `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p positively regulates q iff p regulates q, and p increases the rate or magnitude of execution of q." 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-6601-2165](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "positively regulates"@en 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) InverseOf [positively regulated by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002336) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) SubPropertyOf: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) SubPropertyOf: [causally upstream of, positive effect](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002304) 

- [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212) o [negatively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002212) SubPropertyOf: [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) 

-  Transitive: [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) 


### positively regulates characteristic `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001) 

- [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A relationship that holds between a process and a characteristic in which process (P) positively regulates characteristic (C) iff:&nbsp;P results in an increase in the intensity or magnitude of C." 

- [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "positively regulates characteristic" 

- [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001) SubPropertyOf: [regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019000) 

- [positively regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002213) o [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001) SubPropertyOf: [positively regulates characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0019001) 


### preceded by `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "x is preceded by y if and only if the time point at which y ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which x starts. Formally: x preceded by y iff &omega;(y) &lt;= &alpha;(x), where &alpha; is a function that maps a process to a start point, and &omega; is a function that maps a process to an end point."@en 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "preceded by"@en 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) InverseOf [precedes](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000063) 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) Domain [occurrent](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003) 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) Range [occurrent](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000003) 

- [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) SubPropertyOf: [ends after](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002086) 

- [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) o [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) SubPropertyOf: [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062) 

-  Transitive: [preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062)

### present in taxon `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175`

#### Added
- AnnotationProperty: [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) 

- [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "S present_in_taxon T if some instance of T has some S. This does not means that all instances of T have an S - it may only be certain life stages or sexes that have S" 

- [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0002-6601-2165](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165) 

- [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [OMO_0002000](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0002000) "PREFIX rdfs: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#&gt;
PREFIX owl: &lt;http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#&gt;
PREFIX in_taxon: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162&gt;
PREFIX present_in_taxon: &lt;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175&gt;
CONSTRUCT {
  in_taxon: a owl:ObjectProperty .
  ?witness rdfs:label ?label .
  ?witness rdfs:subClassOf ?x .
  ?witness rdfs:subClassOf [
    a owl:Restriction ;
    owl:onProperty in_taxon: ;
    owl:someValuesFrom ?taxon
  ] .
}
WHERE {
  ?x present_in_taxon: ?taxon .
  BIND(IRI(CONCAT(
    &quot;http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175#&quot;,
    MD5(STR(?x)),
    &quot;-&quot;,
    MD5(STR(?taxon))
  )) as ?witness)
  BIND(CONCAT(STR(?x), &quot; in taxon &quot;, STR(?taxon)) AS ?label)
}" 

- [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "present in taxon"@en

Any class lacking &quot;ready_for_release&quot; should be considered likely to change place in hierarchy, have its definition refined, or be obsoleted in the next release.  Those classes deemed &quot;ready_for_release&quot; will also derived from a chain of ancestor classes that are also &quot;ready_for_release.&quot;"@en

### realizable entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017`

#### Added
- Class: [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the disposition of this piece of metal to conduct electricity."@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the disposition of your blood to coagulate"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the function of your reproductive organs"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of being a doctor"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of this boundary to delineate where Utah and Colorado meet"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A specifically dependent continuant  that inheres in continuant  entities and are not exhibited in full at every time in which it inheres in an entity or group of entities. The exhibition or actualization of a realizable entity is a particular manifestation, functioning or process that occurs under certain circumstances."@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "realizable entity"@en 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) DisjointWith [quality](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000019) 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) SubClassOf [specifically dependent continuant](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020) 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) SubClassOf [characteristic](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/COB_0000502) 

- [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) only [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017)

### regulates `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "p regulates q iff p is causally upstream of q, the execution of p is not constant and varies according to specific conditions, and p influences the rate or magnitude of execution of q due to an effect either on some enabler of q or some enabler of a part of q." 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) [term editor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000117) [0000-0001-7476-6306](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7476-6306)

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) InverseOf [regulated by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002334) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) Domain [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) Range [process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) SubPropertyOf: [causally upstream of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002411) 

- [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) o [has effector activity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025) SubPropertyOf: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211) 

- [directly regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002578) o [directly regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002578) SubPropertyOf: [regulates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002211)

### role `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023`

#### Added
- Class: [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "John&rsquo;s role of husband to Mary is dependent on Mary&rsquo;s role of wife to John, and both are dependent on the object aggregate comprising John and Mary as member parts joined together through the relational quality of being married."@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the priest role"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of a boundary to demarcate two neighboring administrative territories"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of a building in serving as a military target"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of a stone in marking a property boundary"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the role of subject in a clinical trial"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "the student role"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A realizable entity  the manifestation of which brings about some result or end that is not essential to a continuant  in virtue of the kind of thing that it is but that can be served or participated in by that kind of continuant  in some kinds of natural, social or institutional contexts."@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "BFO 2 Reference: One major family of examples of non-rigid universals involves roles, and ontologies developed for corresponding administrative purposes may consist entirely of representatives of entities of this sort. Thus &lsquo;professor&rsquo;, defined as follows,b instance_of professor at t =Def. there is some c, c instance_of professor role &amp; c inheres_in b at t.denotes a non-rigid universal and so also do &lsquo;nurse&rsquo;, &lsquo;student&rsquo;, &lsquo;colonel&rsquo;, &lsquo;taxpayer&rsquo;, and so forth. (These terms are all, in the jargon of philosophy, phase sortals.) By using role terms in definitions, we can create a BFO conformant treatment of such entities drawing on the fact that, while an instance of professor may be simultaneously an instance of trade union member, no instance of the type professor role is also (at any time) an instance of the type trade union member role (any more than any instance of the type color is at any time an instance of the type length).If an ontology of employment positions should be defined in terms of roles following the above pattern, this enables the ontology to do justice to the fact that individuals instantiate the corresponding universals &ndash;  professor, sergeant, nurse &ndash; only during certain phases in their lives."@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "role"@en 

- [role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000023) SubClassOf [realizable entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000017) 


### role of `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "this investigator role is a role of this person"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "a relation between a role and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the role specifically depends on the bearer for its existence"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "A role inheres in its bearer at all times for which the role exists, however the role need not be realized at all the times that the role exists."@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "is role of"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "role_of"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "role of"@en 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) InverseOf [has role](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000087) 

- [role of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000081) SubPropertyOf: [characteristic of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052)

### scatter plot `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184`

#### Added
- Class: [scatter plot](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184) 

- [scatter plot](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A scatterplot is a graph which uses Cartesian coordinates to display values for two variables for a set of data. The data is displayed as a collection of points, each having the value of one variable determining the position on the horizontal axis and the value of the other variable determining the position on the vertical axis."@en 

- [scatter plot](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "scatter plot"@en 

- [scatter plot](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000184) SubClassOf [graph](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000038)

- Individual: [second](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000010) 

- [second](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000010) [textual definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A time unit which is equal to the duration of 9 192 631 770 periods of the radiation corresponding to the transition between the two hyperfine levels of the ground state of the caesium 133 atom."
